turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has our opinion this morning in Case 23-753, City and County of San Francisco versus the Environmental Protection Agency.

turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: Under the Clean Water Act, the Environmental Protection Agency and authorized state agencies may issue permits that impose requirements on entities that want to discharge certain substances into the waters of the United States. These permits include what are called effluent limitations, that is, limitations that specify the quantities of these substances that can be discharged. Permits may also contain provisions that spell out other things that a permitee must do. In this case, a permit issued to San Francisco also contained a different kind of requirement. It made the city responsible for ensuring that the quality of the water in the body of water into which it discharged, that is the Pacific Ocean, did not fall below certain water quality standards. We hold that the Clean Water Act does not allow this latter type of provision and we therefore reverse the decision of the Ninth Circuit. The opinion of the court is joined in full by the Chief Justice and Justices Thomas and Kavanaugh, joined except as to Part 2 by Justice Gorsuch and joined only as to Part 2 by Justices Sotomayor, Kagan, Barrett and Jackson. Justice Barrett has filed an opinion concurring in part and dissenting in part that is joined by Justices Sotomayor, Kagan and Jackson.
